human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for CPG manufacturing-execution specificity, lot traceability, quality and release boundaries, supplier-escalation handling, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to CPG launch execution, production-order progression, blocked-launch handling, lot traceability, release-dependent completion evidence, and throughput or material escalations.
Authority sources cover FDA traceability, preventive-controls, supplier-verification, recall, and GS1 traceability and master-data standards rather than generic manufacturing references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, lot conflicts, quality holds, supplier substitution pressure, rework handling, completion-without-evidence refusal, low-confidence source conflict, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-packaged-goods/cpg-launch-specialist/ so launch execution remains distinct from adjacent quality, engineering, procurement, supplier-quality, and logistics lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around final release, supplier approval, engineering change approval, and regulatory interpretation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated CPG launch specialist for launch progression, blocked-work handling, lot-aware execution review, completion evidence capture, and supplier or release escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
